title: Ghostly Rei appearences
wikitext:
[]: Ghostly Rei appearences?             Quote Post by skikes » Thu Jun 11, 2009 5:16 pm hey all, first post (i think, went to create an account, apparently i already had one) been lurking around here awhile now, thought id join in. I've just been re-watching eva and got to episode 24 when Rei is looking down watching Kaworu in Terminal Dogma and i just wondered whether its ghostly god Rei or Rei III? I'd always just assumed it was the latter. If there's already been discussion on the matter cud someone point me in the right direction.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Jun 11, 2009 5:22 pm Yeah, when he died. She seems to be watching Misato and Ritsuko die in EoE as well. I wonder if there's a connection.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Jun 11, 2009 5:29 pm That Rei in particular is probably the real Rei, because she released the second AT-Field: Episode 24 wrote: Shinji: What's that?! Misato: What's the situation? HYUGA: It's an AT Field! AOBA: The same kind of AT Field that we saw earlier has developed near the barrier around Terminal Dogma! IBUKI: It's entering the barrier! Misato: It can't be! Another Angel?! AOBA: It's no good! We can't confirm that! Oh, no, it disappeared! Misato: Disappeared?! The Angel did?!

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Thu Jun 11, 2009 5:45 pm Yes, but couldn't a ghostly Rei produce an AT Field? I just thought because Kaworu is a part of Uber Rei maybe it was at this point she collected his soul. i know Adam is also part of Uber Rei but i don't think that's sufficient enough to produce the Kaworu that appears to Shinji in EoE. With that thinking Rei didn't need to join with Lilith to produce Uber Rei. But that's another discussion.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Jun 11, 2009 6:15 pm During EoE, Nerv was able to detect AT-Fields from the Lilith/Rei merged entity, but they didn't detect the ghostly apparitions of Rei that popped up all over the place (presumably all over the world). Kaworu got into the Lilith/Rei entity because when Kaworu died, his soul ended up in Adam's embryo in Gendo's hand, which ended up inside Rei III, which merged with Lilith. The collection of souls never started until Lilith/Rei opened up the Chambers of Guf, during 3rd Impact. I don't see any way, given this, that the Rei above Kaworu during episode 24 was one of the ghostly apparitions that appeared during EoE (or episode 1). Also note that Rei's ghostly apparition does not appear during the series at any point when an Angel dies.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Thu Jun 11, 2009 6:20 pm good point. i wish my palm had a soul... wait... no i dont

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Thu Jun 11, 2009 6:22 pm skikes wrote: Yes, but couldn't a ghostly Rei produce an AT Field? I just thought because Kaworu is a part of Uber Rei maybe it was at this point she collected his soul. i know Adam is also part of Uber Rei but i don't think that's sufficient enough to produce the Kaworu that appears to Shinji in EoE. With that thinking Rei didn't need to join with Lilith to produce Uber Rei. But that's another discussion.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Thu Jun 11, 2009 6:29 pm Yeah but i wanted to make the distinction because i wanted to refer to Rei III before she ended up all god like and all... also ive read the article... ive read all the articles. ornette mentioned that his soul couldn't have been collected until the chamber of guf was opened but if Rei III is timeless then couldnt she have went back and collected Kaworu's soul while the chamber was opened. Couldn't the Rei's just not have have expanded their atfs or nerv didnt detect all the other Rei III ATF's because they were too busy becoming lilith soup??? and weren't all ATFs collapsing at that point?

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Thu Jun 11, 2009 8:43 pm Yes, once she merges with Lilith and Adam, Rei 3 does have the power to reach backward in time to collect souls, or else Kaji couldn't be in Instrumenatlity, and he plainly is there. But the Rei who shows up in Terminal Dogma doesn't look like a Ghostly Rei, and and we need to remember that one of the points of episode 24 is that through Kaworu, Rei is learning her true identity. The whole reason she is in this scene is because the revelation that the white giant is Lilith, not Adam, is the last piece of the puzzle. It's neccessary to the plot for it to be Rei 3.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Thu Jun 11, 2009 8:58 pm thewayneiac wrote: Yes, once she merges with Lilith and Adam, Rei 3 does have the power to reach backward in time to collect souls, or else Kaji couldn't be in Instrumenatlity, and he plainly is there. But the Rei who shows up in Terminal Dogma doesn't look like a Ghostly Rei, and and we need to remember that one of the points of episode 24 is that through Kaworu, Rei is learning her true identity. The whole reason she is in this scene is because the revelation that the white giant is Lilith, not Adam, is the last piece of the puzzle. It's neccessary to the plot for it to be Rei 3.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jun 12, 2009 12:22 am Disembodied souls can't project AT Fields. /thread.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Fri Jun 12, 2009 12:33 am AuraTwilight wrote: Disembodied souls can't project AT Fields. /thread.

[]:             Quote Post by Agent_Koopa » Fri Jun 12, 2009 1:20 am AuraTwilight wrote: Disembodied souls can't project AT Fields. /thread.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jun 12, 2009 8:29 pm skikes wrote: but they arent disembodied anythings, its Rei, only one. just capable of existing in many times and places at once.

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Sat Jun 13, 2009 2:41 am thewayneiac wrote: Yes, once she merges with Lilith and Adam, Rei 3 does have the power to reach backward in time to collect souls, or else Kaji couldn't be in Instrumenatlity, and he plainly is there.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Jun 13, 2009 12:42 pm Regardless, Quantum Rei does visit to collect Misato's and Ritsuko's souls before Instrumentality begins, so theoretically, Kaji is just as possible.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Sat Jun 13, 2009 3:16 pm Kaji has been dead for over like a month though. If someone who's been dead that long can come back, then we're at it why don't we bring back Leonardo Da Vinci and Abraham Lincoln, or anyone else who is dead? I think Misato, Asuka, and Ritsuko being tangible during 3I is because they had just died, and thus their souls were still "warm" enough for GNR to harvest. Honestly though, Kaji had been dead for so long that I think he'd be beyond tangibility.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jun 13, 2009 3:27 pm It will depend on how long souls hang around the body -- various magickal traditions make different guesses. We have no hard and fast metabiological data -- but Kaji is present in the EoTV interrogations and Congratulations scene.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Sat Jun 13, 2009 3:47 pm Yeah, but was that actually Kaji's soul, or merely Shinji's vision of him?

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Sat Jun 13, 2009 7:42 pm Timstuff wrote: Kaji has been dead for over like a month though. If someone who's been dead that long can come back, then we're at it why don't we bring back Leonardo Da Vinci and Abraham Lincoln, or anyone else who is dead? I think Misato, Asuka, and Ritsuko being tangible during 3I is because they had just died, and thus their souls were still "warm" enough for GNR to harvest. Honestly though, Kaji had been dead for so long that I think he'd be beyond tangibility.
